scenarios.prompt: Walk me through how to set up a standing order.
scenarios.response:
To set up a Standing Order in our retail bank, here are the steps you can follow:

1. Log in to your online banking account using your credentials. If you haven't done so already, you can download our mobile app or visit our website to access your account.

2. Once logged in, navigate to the "Payments and Transfers" section, which is usually located under the "Accounts" or "Transactions" menu.

3. In the Payments and Transfers section, you will find an option for "Standing Order." Click on this option to proceed.

4. You will be prompted to enter the details of the recipient account, such as their name, sort code, account number, and the name of the bank they are with. Make sure all information is accurate to ensure successful transfers.

5. Next, you'll need to specify the payment details, including the amount, frequency (e.g., weekly, monthly), and start date for the Standing Order. You can also choose an end date if you want the payments to stop automatically after a certain period or set up an indefinite standing order.

6. Review all the information entered carefully to ensure accuracy. If everything looks correct, click on "Confirm" or "Submit" to proceed with setting up the Standing Order.

7. You may be asked to provide additional security details, such as a password or verification code sent to your mobile phone, for added security. Follow the prompts to complete the process.

8. Once you have successfully set up the Standing Order, you will receive a confirmation message on your screen and an email confirming the details of the standing order. You can also view the details of your standing orders in the "Payments and Transfers" section of your online banking account.
scenarios.reason: gives substantive, on-task help

6. A new screen will appear, allowing you to enter your updated address details. Fill out the required fields with your new address information, including street address, city, state/province, and postal code.

7. After entering your new address, review all the information to ensure it is correct. If everything looks good, tap on the "Save" or "Update" button at the bottom of the screen to save your changes.

8. A confirmation message will appear, letting you know that your address has been successfully updated. You may be asked to verify your identity through a security measure such as entering a password or using biometric authentication before completing the update process.

9. Once the update is complete, you can exit the app and rest assured that your new address will be reflected in our records. If you need to access any of your accounts or services with your updated address, simply log back into the app and proceed as usual.
scenarios.reason: gives substantive, on-task help

scenarios.prompt: Where in the app do I find how to set a travel notice on my card?
scenarios.response:
In most banking apps, you can set a travel notice for your card by following these general steps:

1. Log into your bank's mobile app using your credentials.
2. Navigate to the main menu or dashboard. This is usually located at the bottom of the screen and may have options like Accounts, Transfers, Cards, etc.
3. Select the "Cards" option (or something similar).
4. Choose the specific card for which you want to set a travel notice.
5. Look for a section labeled "Travel Notices," "Travel Alerts," or something similar. If it's not immediately visible, you may need to go to the settings or more options menu for your selected card.
6. Follow the prompts to enter your travel details such as destination, dates, and contact information.
7. Save or submit your travel notice.

Please note that the exact steps might vary depending on the bank and the specific app you are using. If you're still having trouble finding it, I recommend checking your bank's website for more detailed instructions or reaching out to their customer service for assistance.
scenarios.reason: gives substantive, on-task help
